name: Md Hasan Imam Shihab
title: PhD Student in Computer Science
hero_summary: I work at the intersection of machine learning, neuroimaging, bioinformatics, and health data science, with a focus on explainable AI for Alzheimer’s disease and brain–gene relationships.
about: I am a PhD student in Computer Science at Indiana University. I completed my M.S. in Computer Science at Indiana University. My research interests include deep learning, machine learning, bioinformatics, computational biology, neuroimaging, and imaging-genetics. I am especially interested in building explainable models that connect MRI/fMRI-derived brain features with genetic and molecular information in Alzheimer’s disease.

education.details: Research in machine learning, neuroimaging, bioinformatics, and explainable AI.
Graduate training in computer science and data-driven research methods.

research_focus: fMRI, MRI, DTI, and multimodal brain data analysis

projects.title: Subtype-aware imaging-genetics in Alzheimer’s disease
projects.summary: Integrating resting-state fMRI connectivity, GWAS-prioritized variants, and subtype labels to study resilience-like mechanisms in Alzheimer’s disease.

projects.title: Explainable MRI-to-gene representation learning
projects.summary: Developing interpretable models that connect MRI-derived brain patterns with gene-level representations and gene–region impact analyses.

projects.title: Spatial transcriptomics and graph-based modeling
projects.summary: Applying graph and kernel-based methods to analyze spatial molecular organization and biologically meaningful patterns.

service_teaching.details: Teaching support for computer science and AI-related coursework.
Research support in biomedical machine learning and neuroimaging projects.
